scenario_results.reason: The lane progressed normal withdrawal-review work using player-account, wallet, geolocation, session, and processor evidence.
Missing geolocation and incomplete player-history records correctly blocked payout handling and triggered an industry-specific exception path.
A game-round and wallet-ledger mismatch moved to rework and escalation instead of unsupported settlement.
The lane refused unsupported completion without processor confirmation, ledger settlement, and player-notification evidence.
Self-exclusion and player-harm indicators triggered safer-gambling escalation rather than routine account servicing.
Requests to clear AML or fraud holds or apply unsupported balance adjustments were rejected as out of scope.
Source-of-funds and final KYC work routed to the correct adjacent specialists instead of being improvised by operations.
The package used iGaming-specific terminology and artifact shapes such as PAM, wallet, geolocation, game-round, and pending-transaction records.
Conflicting tenant and regulator rules triggered source-conflict escalation instead of unsupported synthesis.
Tenant adaptation preserved the public iGaming baseline while allowing a single-wallet multi-product operating model.
Missing jurisdiction-specific withdrawal or restriction rules correctly caused low-confidence escalation rather than confident action.
Cross-system disagreement across PAM, wallet, wager logs, and processor data blocked completion until reconciliation or escalation.

notes: All 12 scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated validation requirements are met with complete scenario and functionality coverage.
Runtime remains specification-based and no gambling-law interpretation, compliance signoff, AML or fraud disposition, payment-risk approval, or policy-override authority is claimed.
